summarization.a: The chart shows the etch rate (nm/cycle) of Al2O3, HfO2, AlNx, TiN and SiO2 against the activation step duration in seconds. The etch rate for all materials starts at a negative value, which corresponds to film formation. The etch rate of all materials increases with time.

a.question: What does a negative etch rate mean?
a.answer: A negative etch rate corresponds to film growth/formation, rather than etching.

a.question: What was the constant modification-step duration used in this experiment?
What causes the lower slope (etch-rate dependence) for HfO2 compared to Al2O3 and AlN3?
a.answer: HfO2 has a higher sputtering threshold.

a.question: The ALE window is seemingly absent for the activation step. Why is this the case according to the authors?
a.answer: It is probably due to the non-monoenergetic ion distribution function. The incline observed in the ALE window for the activation step may hamper reproducibility.
